title: Standardize review submissions to match presentation layer via deterministic mapping
context: EXP-03 revision-2 reviews had divergence: reviewers saw plain-language choices (accept/edit/reject/cannot_decide) but submitted raw metrics, allowing contracts to drift and metrics to be hand-crafted inconsistently.
decision: Reviewers submit exactly what they see: choice + rule text (when applicable) + one sentence reason. A pure deterministic mapper (compileExp03ReviewResponse) is the single translation to canonical metrics (accepted_precise/accepted_edited/rejected/uncompilable). Two mutually exclusive service paths prevent raw metrics from touching revision-2 cases; revision-1 keeps original byte-identical contract.
consequences: Presentation and submission contracts permanently synchronized—no drift possible
Metrics derived, never hand-crafted—choice/text contradictions caught at submission time
Pure mapper enables reproducibility, testability, and audit trail
Versioned handling: revision-1 pilot uses legacy path, revision-2 uses standardized template
CLI exposes only choice/rule/reason—metrics are opaque internal compilation
alternatives_rejected: Allow direct metric submission alongside template—contracts diverge, inconsistency wins
Use flags to conditionally allow raw metrics on revision-2—silent metric leakage possible
Mix raw and standardized paths without mutual exclusion—ambiguous which applies
Store hand-crafted metrics separate from template—dual sources of truth

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Allow direct metric submission alongside template—contracts diverge, inconsistency wins
Use flags to conditionally allow raw metrics on revision-2—silent metric leakage possible
Mix raw and standardized paths without mutual exclusion—ambiguous which applies
Store hand-crafted metrics separate from template—dual sources of truth
